question_filter: opinion/attitude items, 2-7 substantive options, base n>=100, demographics/admin excluded

cells.question: How often do you pay attention to what’s going on in government and politics?
Some people don’t pay much attention to political campaigns. How about you? Would you say that you have been in the political campaigns so far this year?
Do you approve or disapprove of the way the Democratic party handled choosing a new presidential candidate?
Do you approve strongly or not strongly? / Do you disapprove strongly or not strongly?
Now on a diﬀerent topic. Are you registered to vote?
Are you registered to vote? Mark one answer that best describes you.
options: Not registered and do not intend to register
Not registered but intend to register
Registered with no party, or unsure which party

cells.question: The following is the address we currently have on ﬁle for you: Is the following location the one you use for voting?
options: Yes, this is the correct address
No, this is not the correct address

cells.question: What political party are you registered with, if any?
Do you intend to register to vote before the November 5 elections, or do you not intend to register?
options: No, do not intend to register

cells.question: Did you vote in a Presidential primary election or caucus this year?
options: Yes, voted in Democratic primary or caucus
Yes, voted in Republican primary or caucus
No, didn’t vote in a primary or caucus

cells.question: This next question is not about the primary elections and caucuses that were held a few months ago. Instead, we’d like to ask you about the election for President to be held on November 5, in which . Have you already voted in that election, or have you not voted?
Just to be clear, I’m recording that you already voted in the election that is scheduled to take place on November 5. Is that right?
Which one of the following best describes how you voted?
options: Deﬁnitely voted in person at a polling place before election
Deﬁnitely voted by mailing a ballot before election day
Deﬁnitely voted in some other way
Not completely sure whether I voted or not

cells.question: Who did you vote for? , Robert F . Kennedy Jr. (Independent), Cornel West (Independent), Jill Stein (Green), or someone else?
Would you say your preference for this candidate was strong or not strong?
How long before Election day did you decide that you were going to vote the way you did?
Do you intend to vote in the November election for President?
Who do you think you will vote for? , Robert F . Kennedy Jr. (Independent), Cornel West (Independent), Jill Stein (Green), or someone else?
Would you say your preference for this candidate is strong or not strong?
If the election were just between Kamala Harris (Democrat) and Donald Trump (Republican) / Donald Trump (Republican) and Kamala Harris (Democrat), who would you vote for, or would you not vote?
How about the election for the House of Representatives in Washington D.C.? Did you vote for a candidate for the U.S. House of Representatives?
options: Yes, voted for House of Representatives
No, didn’t vote for House of Representatives

cells.question: What is the party of the House candidate you voted for?
How about the election for the House of Representatives in Washington D.C.? Do you intend to vote in the election for the U.S. House of Representatives?
How about the election for the United States Senate? Did you vote for a candidate for the U.S. Senate?
How about the election for the United States Senate? Do you intend to vote in the election for the U.S. Senate?
Who do you think you will vote for?
How about the election for Governor? Do you intend to vote for a candidate for Governor?
How likely is it that you will vote in the general election this November?

Four years ago, in 2020, Joe Biden ran on the Democratic ticket against Donald Trump for the Republicans. We talk to many people who tell us they did not vote. And we talk to a few people who tell us they did vote, who really did not. We can tell they did not vote by checking with oﬃcial government records. What about you? If we check the oﬃcial government voter records, will they show that you voted in the 2020 presidential election, or that you did not vote in that election?
Which one did you vote for in 2020?
Four years ago, in 2020, Joe Biden ran against Donald Trump. Did you vote in that election four years ago?
options: No, didn’t vote for president in 2020

Much better or somewhat better / Much worse or somewhat worse]?

cells.question: Do you favor or oppose the death penalty for persons convicted of murder?
Do you the death penalty for persons convicted of murder strongly or not strongly?
Turning to some questions about America’s role in the world: During the past year, would you say that the United States’ position in the world has grown ?

For the next few items, we would like to know how important you think each one is to the United States maintaining a strong democracy. First, how important is it that news organizations are free to criticize political leaders?
How important is it that the executive, legislative, and judicial branches of government keep one another from having too much power?
How important is it that elected oﬃcials face serious consequences if they engage in misconduct?
How important is it that people agree on basic facts even if they disagree politically?
Would it be helpful, harmful, or neither helpful nor harmful if U.S. presidents could work on the country’s problems without paying attention to what Congress and the courts say?
Do you favor, oppose, or neither favor nor oppose elected oﬃcials restricting journalists’ access to information about government decision-making?
How concerned are you that some people in the government today might want to undermine the news media’s ability to serve as a check on governmental power?
In general, how much trust and conﬁdence do you have in the news media when it comes to reporting the news fully, accurately, and fairly?
Would you prefer a government oﬃcial who compromises to get things done, or who sticks to their principles no matter what?
options: Sticks to their principles no matter what

What is the best way to deal with the problem of urban unrest and rioting? Some say it is more important to use all available force to maintain law and order, no matter what results. Others say it is more important to correct the problems of racism and police violence that give rise to the disturbances. And, of course, other people have opinions in between. On this scale from 1 to 7, where 1 means solve problems of racism and police violence, and 7 means use all available force to maintain law and order, where would you place yourself on this scale, or haven’t you thought much about this?
options: Solve problems of racism and police violence
Use all available force to maintain law and order

We are interested in the guesses people make when they do not know the answer to a question. We will ask you several questions. Some may be easy, but others are meant to be so diﬃcult that you will have to guess. In fact, for some of these questions, if you answer correctly, we will know that you probably looked up the answer. Please do not look up the answers you do not know. Instead, please just make your best guess. Will you please promise to try your best without looking up any answers? Or do you not want to make that promise?
options: I promise to try my best without looking up any answers
I do not want to make that promise
